text.title: Platform Execution: Meta Experiments, Google Ads, Bayesian Verdict, Agent Gotchas
text.summary: How to run tests on Meta and Google Ads platforms, when to use Bayesian vs. frequentist significance, and agent-specific failure modes.

section.title: Meta Experiments
section.p: Meta's Experiments tool (Ads Manager → Experiments → A/B Test) guarantees equal traffic split. Supports testing: audiences, creatives, placements, optimization goals. Minimum $200/variant. Meta declares a winner automatically when significance is reached.

rule.statement: Use Meta's Experiments tool for all creative tests when budget allows; default ad rotation does not guarantee equal split and will bias results toward the algorithm's early favorite.
rule.rationale: Meta's algorithm begins optimizing within hours of launch. Without the Experiments tool, the platform reallocates spend toward whichever variant performs better early, breaking the equal-split assumption that makes frequentist analysis valid.

section.title: Google Ads Experiments
section.p: Google Ads Experiments (Campaigns → Experiments → Ad Variations): create a variation, set traffic split percentage (50/50 for clean test), set duration, monitor, apply winner. For search ads: test one ad element at a time within an ad group. Review after statistical significance is reached in platform reporting.

section.title: Bayesian Verdict
section.p: For off-platform analysis, use Bayesian Beta-binomial testing: P(variant > control) > 0.95 = winner; < 0.05 = control wins; between 0.05 and 0.95 = inconclusive, extend the test or accept the uncertainty.
section.reference: Bayesian Beta-binomial verdict function — p_variant_better, expected lift, winner classification

antipattern.description: Mixing significance methods — using Meta's platform significance for some tests and scipy.stats.ttest_ind for others.
antipattern.reason: Different statistical engines compute significance differently. Mixing methods makes tests incomparable and can produce contradictory verdicts on the same data.

rule.statement: Never auto-promote a variant to winner without human sign-off, even when the significance threshold is reached.
rule.rationale: A variant may win on CTR while losing on CPA. The agent's job is to compute and report the verdict; the human decides whether to apply it and which metric matters most for the business decision.

list.item: Require sample size computation before launching any test; refuse to verdict until threshold is met.
When variant outperforms by more than 50%, check for data leakage (audience overlap, tracking gap) before reporting as a winner.
Inconclusive tests must extend, not restart — restarting resets the sample and wastes the data already collected.
Cross-currency or cross-region tests need revenue normalized to a base currency before computing ROAS lift.
Running 5+ concurrent tests on the same audience inflates false positive rate — apply Bonferroni correction or limit concurrent tests to 3 per audience.
